hypothesis: On M1/aarch64 the per-block lut_huffman litlen table (asm.lut_litlen) is BUILT every block-header in read_header but, on the T1 clean-decode path, NEVER READ (the clean contig path is engine-A consume_first_decode::decode_huffman_fastloop_bounded_pipelined + its own flat_litlen LitLenTable; lut_litlen.decode_prefilled is the reader and it is the inverse cfg of engine-A's compile gate).

data_flow_confirmation.instrument: feature=lut-count, GZIPPY_LUT_COUNT=1 (lut_huffman::litlen_count: BUILDS/CACHE_HITS/READS atomics; non-inert Gate-0)
data_flow_confirmation.note: LitLenTable (flat_litlen, libdeflate_entry) is built from the raw code lengths, NOT from lut_huffman output. The two are independent tables. Redundancy CONFIRMED at T1.
data_flow_confirmation.interpretation: At T1 the table was built 3286x and read 0x -> redundant build eliminated (3286->0). At T10 the marker/ring path reads it 22.18M x; the after build keeps building lazily exactly where read (1179 builds = marker-path blocks), only the never-read clean-path eager builds are retired. reads unchanged (~22.18M, run-variance only).

removal_oracle.change: marker_inflate.rs read_header: the eager per-block build_huffman_luts_for_block() is now cfg-gated to exactly where the clean path READS lut_litlen, i.e. any(legacy-isal-x86, all(pure_inflate_decode, asm-kernel, x86_64)) -- the exact inverse of decode_clean_into_contig's engine-A gate not(all(asm-kernel, x86_64)). On aarch64 (and x86 asm-off) the build is left to the lazy latch-guarded readers (read_internal_compressed_specialized:2005, engine-B fallback:3595).
removal_oracle.byte_exact_rationale: Build is a deterministic function of code lengths; it is relocated/skipped, never changed. Validity check preserved: clean path via LitLenTable::build (engine-A ensure_flat_litlen), fallback paths via lazy build_huffman_luts_for_block()?.

load_immune_instruction_reduction.metric: instructions retired (/usr/bin/time -l, kpc) -- load-immune; interleaved N=9, T1 silesia
load_immune_instruction_reduction.significance: delta 93.3M >> spread ~4M (>20x). Exceeds the ~58M lut_build-phase estimate because whole-program also drops the table memset/short+long fill traffic of the 3286 eliminated builds.

byte_exactness.cargo_test: 952 passed / 0 failed (lib, --features pure-rust-inflate); lut_huffman proptest + inflate corpus + decompress + marker differentials all pass

x86_unaffected.static_cfg_proof: On x86_64 pure-rust-inflate (asm-kernel pulled ON), the eager-build cfg evaluates IDENTICALLY before/after: BEFORE second arm 'pure_inflate_decode'=true; AFTER second arm 'all(pure_inflate_decode, asm-kernel, x86_64)'=true. The legacy isal-x86 arm is unchanged. The cfg only flips true->false on aarch64 and on x86 asm-OFF -- both non-asm clean-path configs that decode via flat_litlen and never read lut_litlen.
x86_unaffected.functional_x86_run: CONFIRMED on neurotic (real GenuineIntel x86_64, path=ParallelSM, --no-default-features --features pure-rust-inflate, RUSTFLAGS=-C target-cpu=native). Variant=ec74ae75 (lever 20758d5c + this guard test); base=3d590738 (lever's direct parent). The earlier x86_64-apple-darwin cross-build failure ('inline assembly requires more registers') was a pre-existing M1-cross limitation, not this change.

x86_confirm_neurotic.variant_sha_eq_oracle: BYTE-EXACT on silesia/logs/monorepo at T1 and T4 (silesia 028bd002..., logs 35c38d94..., monorepo 0dd50d07...)
x86_confirm_neurotic.base_sha_eq_oracle: BYTE-EXACT on the same 9 cells; base decode output == variant decode output == gzip -dc oracle (identical x86 decode behavior)
x86_confirm_neurotic.cfg_inert_on_x86_lut_counts: variant (lever cfg, +lut-count) silesia T1: builds=3286 reads=6593; base-form-cfg rebuild (eager-build cfg reverted to bare pure_inflate_decode, +lut-count) silesia T1: builds=3286 reads=6593 -- IDENTICAL. T4: reads=15661095 identical, builds 3305 vs 3306 (1-block cache-eviction run variance). Both BUILD>0 AND READ>0 on x86 => the eager build still runs and lut_litlen is still read on the x86 clean path; the lever's skip never applies on x86.
x86_confirm_neurotic.wall_t1_silesia_dev_null_interleaved_n9: variant min=0.67s, base min=0.66s, variant/base min-ratio=1.0152; Delta(0.01s) < inter-run spread(~0.02s/3%) => TIE, no x86 regression. (Expected: on x86 the two binaries do byte-identical work.)

x86_unaffected.x86_keeps_build_and_read: x86 production (asm-kernel) still eager-builds in read_header AND reads via run_contig/decode_prefilled; the +7.7% multisym-TRIPLE path is untouched (litlen_multisym_flag still TRIPLE on x86). CONFIRMED empirically on neurotic: T1 builds=3286 reads=6593 (see x86_confirm_neurotic).

wall_status: HYPOTHESIS until wall-gated on a quiet box (fulcrum abmeasure/optgate on Linux x86/arm). Instruction reduction is load-immune and gated; wall is the downstream check.
